_note: POC IELTS listening test sourced from VOA Learning English (public-domain US-government work). Audio streamed from VOA's CDN; questions are original IP. Run: php artisan ingest:voa-listening database/seeders/data/voa_listening_poc.json --force
title: VOA POC — Mixed Topics Listening Test 01
content: 4-section IELTS-style listening test built on public-domain VOA Learning English episodes. Section 1: vocabulary explanation (beach/coast/shore). Section 2: gardening monologue. Section 3: space-mission feature. Section 4: education feature.
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: The lesson is about the words beach, coast and ________.
A ________ is generally covered with sand or small stones along the edge of the sea.
The ________ refers to the long stretch of land that meets the sea.
The word 'shore' can be used for land next to a sea, lake or ________.
Which word is most commonly used when talking about vacations and swimming?
Which word is most often used in geographical or political descriptions of a country?
The expression 'on the ________' often appears in literature meaning beside the water.
The phrase 'East ________ of the United States' is a common geographical reference.
Which sentence is the most natural?
options: A. We are going to the beach this weekend.
B. We are going to the coast this weekend for sunbathing.
C. We are going to the shore this weekend to swim.
D. All three are equally natural.
questions.answer: A. We are going to the beach this weekend.

questions.question: The three words have related meanings but are used in different ________.
The topic of the talk is using everyday containers to start ________.
Egg ________ make excellent biodegradable seed-starting pots.
Empty ________ rolls cut in half can be used as small seedling tubes.
What is the main advantage of using recycled household containers?
Containers must have ________ holes so excess water can escape.
Yogurt cups and plastic ________ bottles cut in half can also be reused.
Why are biodegradable containers preferred for some seedlings?
options: A. They can be planted directly in the ground
questions.answer: A. They can be planted directly in the ground

questions.question: Seedlings should be kept in a warm and well-________ place.
Once true leaves appear the seedlings can be moved into the ________.
Which container type is NOT suggested by the speaker?
The feature discusses private ________ missions in 2025.
Two private American companies attempted lunar ________ this year.
Which company's lander successfully reached the Moon upright?
The Intuitive Machines lander reportedly fell on its ________ after touchdown.
Private moon missions are supported by NASA's ________ Lunar Payload Services program.
What is one main benefit of involving private companies in lunar missions?
options: A. Lower cost compared with government-only missions
D. Eliminating the need for astronauts
questions.answer: A. Lower cost compared with government-only missions

questions.question: Each lander carried scientific ________ to study the lunar surface.
The south ________ of the Moon is a key target for future missions due to suspected water ice.
What is the biggest challenge for private lunar landers?
options: A. Soft, controlled landing on the lunar surface
questions.answer: A. Soft, controlled landing on the lunar surface

questions.question: NASA's broader plan to return humans to the Moon is called the ________ program.
The feature is about scientists who have ________ and the barriers they face at work.
Many laboratory spaces are not ________ for people who use wheelchairs.
What change do scientists with disabilities most commonly seek?
options: A. More accessible lab equipment and workspaces
questions.answer: A. More accessible lab equipment and workspaces

questions.question: Assistive ________ such as screen readers and speech-to-text tools are increasingly used.
Some researchers say funding agencies should include ________ as a criterion in grant reviews.
Which group is mentioned as historically under-represented in science?
Conferences can be made more inclusive by offering ________ language interpretation.
Lack of ________ in fieldwork sites limits opportunities for disabled scientists.
What does AI-powered assistive technology MOST help disabled researchers do?
options: A. Read, write and communicate scientific work
questions.answer: A. Read, write and communicate scientific work

questions.question: Improving inclusion in science will, the speakers argue, lead to better and more diverse ________.
